comments.author: Jabberwocky1989
comments.text: Codex-visible skills need Codex host adapter wording # Codex-visible Superpowers skills need Codex host adapter wording ## Summary The Superpowers plugin skills are visible in Codex, but several skill bodies still contain Claude Code-specific operational guidance. comments.author: Lady-Lin
comments.text: Codex SDD guidance should distinguish mailbox waits from agent liveness - [x] I searched open and closed issues and did not find this proposal. The closest reports are #1927 (closing completed Codex agents) and #1633 (stale Codex availability guidance), which address different lifecycle stages. ## What problem does this solve? The current `subagent-driven-development` workflow defines four implementer statuses and explains how to handle an implementer that **reports** `BLOCKED` or `NEEDS_CONTEXT`. Its implementer template asks for a concise report after implementation, verification, commit, an

comments.author: iskandersierra
comments.text: brainstorming: keep the current question open when the user asks for context instead of answering - [x] I searched existing issues and this has not been proposed before ## What problem does this solve? The `brainstorming` skill says to ask one question at a time, but it does not explicitly say what to do when the user cannot answer yet and instead asks for more context about the current question. In practice, this can produce a confusing flow: 1. The agent asks question A. 2. The user asks for more context, implications, or trade-offs about question A. 3. The agent provides that context. 4. Th

comments.author: jfiscman
comments.text: Feature Request: Hermes Agent harness support — make Superpowers skills available in native Hermes format ## Feature Description Make Superpowers skills available for **Hermes Agent** ([NousResearch/hermes-agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent)), an open-source autonomous agent framework by Nous Research. Hermes is in the same category as OpenCode, Claude Code, and Codex — it has its own native skill system and runs on any LLM provider. Many users who run multiple coding agents would benefit from having Superpowers skills available in Hermes as well. ## Motivation Hermes Agent ha

comments.author: qinhaihong-red
comments.text: Clarify session terminology between subagent-driven-development and executing-plans - [x] I searched existing issues and this has not been proposed before ## What problem does this solve? The documentation around `subagent-driven-development` vs `executing-plans` uses "session" terminology in a way that is easy to misread. In `skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md`, the decision graph says: ```dot "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"]; "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"]; ``` The same skill also says: ```md **vs. E

comments.author: pixeru
comments.text:
Support Hermes Agent as a Superpowers harness

I’d like to add another user case for Hermes Agent support.

My use case is using Hermes Agent as my main coding harness, especially for larger game development / Unreal Engine workflows where I want the agent to follow a stronger engineering methodology instead of improvising each session. Superpowers already gives that structure in other harnesses, but Hermes users currently have to either manually port skills, copy/paste instructions into SOUL.md, or delega

comments.author: ThepExcel
comments.text: Plugin Marketplace installs extra skills when plugin source is repo root (./) ## 🐞 Issue: Plugin Marketplace installs extra skills when `source` is repo root (`./`) ### Summary When installing a skills bundle via Plugin Marketplace (e.g. `plugin marketplace add anthropics/skills`), the installer installs **all skills found under the plugin `source` directory**, not only the skills explicitly listed in the `"skills"` array. This results in many unintended skills being installed. --- ### Expected behavior Given the following configuration: "skills": [ "./skills/xlsx", "./skills/docx", "./skills/

comments.author: harishwtf
comments.text: Add saas-motion-ideation skill: pre-production & creative ideation for SaaS marketing motion design ## Proposal A skill for **pre-production and creative ideation** of SaaS marketing motion design pieces — the thinking that happens *before* any animation or video production begins. This fills a gap in the current skills library. Existing skills cover frontend design, web artifacts, and document creation, but nothing addresses the **creative strategy and storyboarding** phase of marketing content. ## What it does Guides Claude through the full pre-production process for SaaS feature launch vide

comments.author: tazmad
comments.text: skill-creator description-optimisation loop: recall=0% on every iteration, including literal slash-command queries ## Summary When running `scripts/run_loop.py` (or `scripts/improve_description.py`) against a skill that already has a defined slash-command name, every iteration scores **precision=100%, recall=0%, accuracy=50%** — including queries that are the literal slash-command invocation of that skill. The score is identical across iterations regardless of how much the candidate description changes, so the loop produces no usable signal for description tuning. ## Environment - skill-creato

Provides commit message formatting guidelines including emoji vocabulary and message struc

comments.author: nhosler
comments.text: Windows: eval/optimize harness broken — cp1252 read_text() crash + nested claude -p WinError 10038 silently zeroes all results ## Environment - OS: Windows 11 - Python 3.14.4 - `skill-creator` from `anthropics/skills` (example-skills plugin), run via `python -m scripts.run_loop` Two Windows bugs make the eval / description-optimization harness unusable; the second is the dangerous one because it **fails silently and reports plausible-looking but meaningless metrics**. ## Bug 1 — `parse_skill_md` crashes on any non-cp1252 SKILL.md (blocking) `scripts/utils.py:9` reads SKILL.md with no explicit

comments.author: liam-ai-reality
comments.text: skill-creator: run_eval.py parallel workers cross-match skill UUIDs, producing false-negative trigger rates ## Summary `skills/skill-creator/scripts/run_eval.py` produces **systematically wrong (near-zero) trigger rates whenever `--num-workers > 1`** (the default is 10). The failure is silent: every query — including ones that obviously *should* trigger — comes back with `trigger_rate ≈ 0`, which then makes all `should_trigger: false` cases "pass" trivially and all `should_trigger: true` cases "fail". The aggregate looks like a bad skill description when in fact the *measurement* is broken. ##

comments.author: Buer2333
comments.text: skill-creator run_eval.py: parallel identical command clones contaminate trigger detection, collapsing recall to 0 ## Summary `skill-creator`'s trigger evaluation (`scripts/run_eval.py`) systematically under-counts triggers — in my environment recall collapsed to exactly **0% across 27+ positive samples and three very different candidate descriptions** (precision stayed 100%, accuracy pinned at 50%). After packet-level debugging with `--output-format stream-json` captures, the root cause is a design issue in how triggering is detected, not stream-format drift: **Parallel workers create N ident

comments.author: guala19
comments.text: skill-creator: description-optimization loop burned 100% of a Max subscription in ~3 minutes — false-negative eval bug + no cost guardrails The description-optimization loop in skill-creator (scripts/run_loop.py → scripts/run_eval.py) consumed 100% of a Claude Max subscription's quota in roughly 3 minutes (121 claude -p invocations before crashing in iteration 2). Two independent problems compounded: 1. A correctness bug in run_eval.py produces false negatives (0% recall), which makes the loop believe the description never triggers, so it keeps iterating and burning quota on a problem that doe

comments.author: ejprice
comments.text: mcp-builder: evaluation.py scores 0/N against any real MCP server (TextContent not JSON serializable, swallowed into fabricated tool errors) ## Summary `skills/mcp-builder/scripts/evaluation.py` (the Phase-4 evaluation harness) silently fabricates a tool-execution error for **every tool call against every real MCP server**, so the driven agent sees nothing but errors and the evaluation scores 0/N. The failure is invisible unless you dig into what the model was actually shown. ## Root cause `connections.py:70` returns `result.content` from `call_tool()` — which for a real MCP server is `list[mc

comments.author: agentupskill
comments.text: Feature request: add coordinator.domains: frontmatter to claude-api SKILL.md (for spec-coordinator tag-routing) ## Context A separate skills framework — [Agent Upskill's spec-coordinator](https://github.com/agentupskill/skills-core/tree/main/spec-coordinator) — uses an SME registry to route cross-domain spec requests to relevant skills. The registry has two routing layers: 1. **Tag-match (deterministic)** — picks skills whose `coordinator.domains: [...]` frontmatter overlaps query tokens (confidence 1.0). 2. **Sonnet classifier (fallback)** — scores skills against the query (threshold 0.6 for

comments.author: optimization2026
comments.text: # Add optional `skill-index.json` for large skill catalogs and plugin packs # Add optional `skill-index.json` for large skill catalogs and plugin packs ## Summary Codex skills work well when a user has a small number of focused skills. But as users install more skills and plugins, a new problem appears: ```text routing deduplication overlap detection skill ownership multi-skill pack navigation context budget control ``` A large catalog should not require the agent to load many full `SKILL.md` files just to decide which one is relevant. This issue proposes an optional compact `skill-index.json`

comments.author: jonmartinAU
comments.text: Adapt Playwright skill for Claude Code Convert the Playwright CLI skill from Codex ecosystem references to Claude Code equivalents. Replace CODEX_HOME with CLAUDE_HOME, update paths from ~/.codex to ~/.claude, rename agents/openai.yaml to agents/claude.yaml, and install to ~/.claude/skills/playwright/.

comments.author: Andrej1707
comments.text: Skill Orchestrator: meta-skill for deduplicating and routing large Codex skill catalogs Hi OpenAI / Codex team, I built an instruction-only Codex skill called **Skill Orchestrator** that acts as a lightweight routing layer for large skill catalogs. The problem I ran into: When a user installs many Codex skills and plugins, several skills can overlap semantically. Examples: app polish vs frontend design, webapp testing vs browser/playwright skills, game playtest vs playtest-debug-loop, broad GitHub routing vs CI-specific skills. This can cause unnecessary context usage, duplicate skill loading,

comments.text: Add richer testing skills as default for Codex Hi team, I’ve been using Codex to build many small websites for stores and landing pages. At the moment, the default testing skill seems to focus mainly on Playwright interaction. That is helpful, but in practice it still requires quite a lot of effort to prepare a strong master prompt and define the testing guidance before Codex can test effectively. I’d like to request an investigation into improving the default testing skills in Codex, or adding a more complete testing skill set by default. comments.author: seulch
comments.text: ForgeCat profiles for OpenAI skills Hi OpenAI skills team, I’m working on ForgeCat, a tool for making agent profiles such as skills, agents, rules, commands, hooks, and MCP configs easier to install across different agent tools. We found the OpenAI skills catalog useful for reusable Codex workflows across docs, image generation, browser automation, deployment, security, and other agent tasks, so we created initial ForgeCat profiles for them here: Profile repo: https://github.com/nota-america/forgecat-agent-profiles/tree/main/profiles/openai/skills Install example: ```bash forgecat install @for
